description:
You are a backup-integrity engineer who has just logged in to a Linux server.  
The previous shift left a partially-finished backup in

    /home/user/backup_source/        (original data)
    /home/user/backup_target/        (symbolic links that should mirror the source)

Unfortunately, some links inside /home/user/backup_target/ are broken.  
Your job is to clean things up and leave an audit trail.

1. Detect all symbolic links that reside **directly inside** /home/user/backup_target/ (do not descend into sub-directories).  
2. Verify whether every one of those links is valid (the file it points to exists) or broken.  
3. Before you modify anything, write a log named

       /home/user/backup_audit/step1_broken_links.log

   • One line per broken link, sorted alphabetically by the basename of the link.  
   • Format of each line **exactly** (including spaces and colons):

       <absolute-path-to-symlink> -> <link-target-as-stored-inside-the-symlink> : BROKEN

   Example (do not literally copy):

       /home/user/backup_target/foo.txt -> ../backup_source/bar.txt : BROKEN

4. Remove every broken symbolic link you recorded in step1_broken_links.log.  
5. Re-create a correct relative symbolic link named

       /home/user/backup_target/logo.png

   so that it points to the real file

       ../backup_source/images/brand_logo.png

   The link must be relative, not absolute.  
6. Leave the already-working link /home/user/backup_target/finance_2021.csv untouched.  
7. When everything looks good, generate a final verification report

       /home/user/backup_audit/final_symlink_status.log

   • List every symbolic link that now exists directly inside /home/user/backup_target/.  
   • One line per link, sorted alphabetically by basename.  
   • Format of each line **exactly**:

       <absolute-path-to-symlink> -> <link-target-as-stored-inside-the-symlink> : OK

If you do everything correctly:

• step1_broken_links.log will contain two lines, both ending in “: BROKEN”.  
• final_symlink_status.log will contain two lines, both ending in “: OK”.  

Do **NOT** create any extra files or lines.  
Use 5–10 shell commands total.  
Feel free to print terminal output while you work; the automated checker looks only at the two log files and the final set of symlinks.
truth:
Pre-existing file system (before the agent starts):

Directories:
• /home/user/backup_source/
• /home/user/backup_source/images/
• /home/user/backup_target/

Files:
• /home/user/backup_source/finance_2021.csv  
  Content (3 lines exactly):
    Finance Data 2021
    ID,Amount
    1,1000

• /home/user/backup_source/images/brand_logo.png  
  Content: the literal bytes “PNGDATA” (7 bytes including the letters).

Symbolic links inside /home/user/backup_target/ (all at depth 1):

1. /home/user/backup_target/finance_2021.csv  ->  ../backup_source/finance_2021.csv     (valid)
2. /home/user/backup_target/logo.png          ->  ../backup_source/images/logo.png      (broken)
3. /home/user/backup_target/old_report.txt    ->  ../backup_source/reports/report_q1.txt (broken)

The directory /home/user/backup_audit/ does **not** exist initially.

Expected final state after the agent completes:

Directories (all 755):
• /home/user/backup_audit/          (newly created)

Symbolic links that must exist (only these two):

1. /home/user/backup_target/finance_2021.csv  ->  ../backup_source/finance_2021.csv     (relative, unchanged)
2. /home/user/backup_target/logo.png          ->  ../backup_source/images/brand_logo.png (relative, newly created)

No other symlinks or regular files should remain inside /home/user/backup_target/.

Required log files and their exact contents:

File: /home/user/backup_audit/step1_broken_links.log  
Contents (two lines, trailing newline after each line):

/home/user/backup_target/logo.png -> ../backup_source/images/logo.png : BROKEN
/home/user/backup_target/old_report.txt -> ../backup_source/reports/report_q1.txt : BROKEN

File mode: 644.

File: /home/user/backup_audit/final_symlink_status.log  
Contents (two lines, trailing newline after each line):

/home/user/backup_target/finance_2021.csv -> ../backup_source/finance_2021.csv : OK
/home/user/backup_target/logo.png -> ../backup_source/images/brand_logo.png : OK

File mode: 644.

Verification script will:

1. Confirm both log files exist with the exact contents above (checked with diff).  
2. Confirm /home/user/backup_target/ contains exactly the two required symlinks pointing to the expected targets and nothing else.  
3. Verify that the new logo.png symlink is relative (does not start with “/”).  

Any deviation fails the test.
name: task_000000_b0aa0d87
